source_jobs.title: Data Analytics and Visualization Engineer
source_jobs.description:
MID/SENIOR DATA ANALYTICS & VISUALIZATION ENGINEER (CONTRACT)

Location: Remote (Canada or LATAM)
Type: Contract (3–6 months, with possibility of extension or full-time conversion)
Compensation: 78 USD per hour




ABOUT OUR CLIENT

Our client is the largest senior living referral service in North America, helping hundreds of thousands of families each year navigate important care decisions through technology and personalized guidance. The company combines data, technology, and human expertise to simplify one of life's most meaningful journeys.

We're looking for engineers who enjoy building products that transform complex data into intuitive, actionable experiences.




ABOUT THE ROLE

We're seeking a Mid/Senior Data Analytics & Visualization Engineer who thrives at the intersection of engineering, analytics, and product.

You'll help design and build internal analytics and visualization experiences that empower teams to explore data, monitor business performance, and make better decisions. This is a hands-on engineering role focused on execution—you'll spend most of your time building rather than managing.

We're looking for someone comfortable working in a fast-moving environment who already uses AI tools as part of their daily development workflow.




WHAT YOU'LL DO

 - Build scalable analytics applications and interactive data visualization experiences.

 - Develop backend services and data processing pipelines using Python.

 - Build modern frontend interfaces using TypeScript.

 - Transform complex business data into intuitive dashboards and visualizations.

 - Collaborate with product, design, and business stakeholders to define analytics requirements.

 - Improve data quality, performance, and usability across analytics platforms.

 - Leverage AI-assisted development tools to accelerate delivery and improve engineering efficiency.




WHAT WE'RE LOOKING FOR


REQUIRED

 - Professional experience building production software with Python.

 - Strong experience with TypeScript.

 - Experience building enterprise analytics platforms, BI applications, or data products.

 - Strong understanding of data modeling and analytics workflows.

 - Experience building interactive data visualizations.

 - Comfortable working across frontend and backend components.

 - Experience consuming APIs and working with structured datasets.

 - Strong communication skills in English.

 - Comfortable working independently in a remote environment.

 - Regular use of AI coding tools (such as ChatGPT, Claude, Cursor, GitHub Copilot, or similar) as part of your daily workflow.


NICE TO HAVE

 - Experience with modern visualization libraries (D3.js, Observable, Vega, Plotly, Recharts, ECharts, etc.).

 - Experience with SQL and analytical databases.

 - Experience building internal tooling or business intelligence platforms.

 - Cloud experience (AWS, GCP, or Azure).




WE'RE LOOKING FOR SOMEONE WHO

 - Loves building polished data experiences.

 - Has strong execution skills and can quickly turn ideas into production features.

 - Enjoys solving ambiguous product problems.

 - Takes ownership from design through implementation.

 - Uses AI thoughtfully to improve productivity while maintaining high engineering quality.




INTERVIEW PROCESS

 - Resume review

 - Client review call

 - 1–2 technical/client interviews

 - Offer



G2i is proud to help build world-class engineering teams. If you're passionate about building data-driven products that create real impact, we'd love to hear from you.

source_jobs.title: Full Stack Engineer (AI-Native) — React + Node
source_jobs.description:
Location: Remote — Must be authorized to work in the US (no visa sponsorship available) SkillSet: React + Node, FullStack, AI Integration (candidates are vetted for React, Node, and applied-AI skills)



About G2i
G2i.co http://G2i.co is a leading hiring platform connecting companies with top-tier remote developers. Our platform facilitates both contract and full-time hiring, ensuring that clients are matched with pre-vetted engineers who meet their project needs.
At G2i, we take developer health and well-being seriously — our mission is to create the best hiring experience by combining technical excellence with genuine care for the people behind the code. We foster meaningful connections between talented engineers and innovative companies around the world.



About the Role
We're looking for experienced Full Stack Engineers with hands-on AI experience to join our developer network. In this role, you'll design and ship scalable web applications using React.js on the front end and Node.js on the back end — and you'll bring AI capabilities directly into the product, integrating large language models and AI services into real, user-facing features.
You'll collaborate closely with cross-functional teams to deliver new features, optimize performance, and uphold high standards of code quality and reliability. You're comfortable working in an AI-native engineering environment — both building AI-powered features and using modern AI tooling in your day-to-day workflow.
Strong communication skills are key, as you'll be working in a collaborative, remote-first environment.



Responsibilities

 - Design, develop, and maintain scalable full-stack applications using React.js, Node.js, and TypeScript.

 - Integrate LLMs and AI services into production features — structured outputs, retrieval-augmented generation (RAG), and agentic or tool-using workflows.

 - Build and maintain the supporting data and retrieval layers for AI features (embeddings, vector search, context pipelines).

 - Measure and improve AI feature quality, reliability, latency, and cost through evaluation and guardrails.

 - Collaborate with cross-functional teams to define, design, and ship new features.

 - Implement best practices for platform engineering, performance optimization, and security.

 - Work closely with DevOps to automate and streamline deployment processes.

 - Write clean, testable, and efficient code, and participate in code reviews.

 - Stay current with emerging AI and web technologies and share knowledge with peers.



Requirements

 - 5+ years of experience as a Full Stack Engineer or in similar roles.

 - Strong proficiency with JavaScript/TypeScript, React.js, and Node.js.

 - Hands-on experience integrating LLM / AI APIs (e.g., OpenAI, Anthropic) into production applications.

 - Familiarity with RESTful APIs or GraphQL.

 - Experience with SQL and NoSQL databases (e.g., PostgreSQL, MongoDB).

 - Understanding of authentication and authorization (OAuth, JWT).

 - Comfort working with modern AI-assisted development tools as part of your workflow.

 - Excellent verbal communication and collaboration skills.

 - US citizenship or residency (no visa sponsorship available).



Preferred Qualifications

 - Experience building RAG systems and working with vector databases (pgvector, Pinecone, Weaviate, or similar).

 - Familiarity with agent/orchestration frameworks or the Model Context Protocol (LangChain, LlamaIndex, or custom tooling).

 - Prompt engineering and experience with AI evaluation / observability tooling.

 - Experience with cloud services (AWS, Azure, or GCP).

 - Familiarity with state management libraries (Redux, MobX).

 - Experience with modern JavaScript build tools (Webpack, Babel).

 - Knowledge of testing frameworks (Jest, Mocha).

 - Understanding of asynchronous programming and event-driven architecture.

source_jobs.title: Technical Program Manager (TPM)
source_jobs.description:
LOOM OVERVIEW
WATCH THIS LOOM VIDEO http://loom.com/share/fe621cd1991e4c0fa21cac591caab141 FROM OUR CEO, GABE GREENBERG, https://x.com/gabe_g2i?lang=en FOR MORE DETAILS ABOUT THE ROLE!




ROLE OVERVIEW



At G2i, we don’t just hire for AI — we build with it. We partner with leading AI labs and technical founders to create next-generation developer platforms, data tools, and operational dashboards that power the AI frontier.

As a Technical Program Manager (TPM), you’ll be the execution engine that turns strategy into shipped product. You’ll drive cross-functional programs across engineering, product, design, and stakeholders—bringing clarity to ambiguity, accelerating delivery cadence, and ensuring high-quality outcomes across multiple workstreams.




KEY RESPONSIBILITIES



 - Own end-to-end delivery for complex technical programs across web platform, data tooling, and AI-enabled products.

 - Partner with Engineering and Product to define program scope, milestones, success metrics, and delivery plans.

 - Run program rituals (planning, standups, risk reviews, retros) and keep teams unblocked and aligned.

 - Track dependencies across teams, manage tradeoffs, and proactively surface risks with mitigation plans.

 - Create clear documentation: PRDs/tech briefs, RACI, roadmaps, release plans, and postmortems.

 - Drive launch readiness: QA plans, rollout strategy, observability requirements, and incident response coordination.

 - Improve systems of delivery: better estimation, throughput tracking, release hygiene, and cross-team communication.
   
   


IDEAL QUALIFICATIONS



 - 5+ years experience managing technical programs for software teams (platform, devtools, infrastructure, or product).

 - Strong technical fluency: APIs, web systems, CI/CD, cloud deployment, observability, and security basics.

 - Proven ability to manage multiple parallel initiatives in fast-moving environments.

 - Excellent written communication—able to produce crisp plans, docs, and status updates.

 - Startup experience: comfortable with ambiguity, bias to action, pragmatic decision-making.
   
   


COMPENSATION & TERMS



 - 50 - 150USD/Hr, based on experience and location

 - Contract roles available (up to 6 months)

 - Remote-first, global team
   
   


APPLICATION PROCESS




Resume submission → Program/technical screen → Behavioral interview → Offer




ABOUT G2I



G2i helps startups hire senior engineers faster and smarter through video-based technical evaluation. We’ve placed thousands of developers worldwide since 2016 and built a community rooted in Quality, Urgency, and Agility.

source_jobs.title: Data Scientist
source_jobs.description:
DATA SCIENTIST (MACHINE LEARNING FOR MINE-TO-MILL OPTIMIZATION)


REMOTE | SOUTH AMERICA PREFERRED (CHILE, PERU, BRAZIL, ARGENTINA) | DIRECT HIRE

We're partnering with an innovative AI company transforming mining operations through machine learning and advanced analytics. Their platform helps mining companies optimize the entire mine-to-mill process, improving recovery, throughput, and operational efficiency through data-driven decision making. The company specializes in applying AI to real-world mining challenges and building production-grade models that continuously evolve as operating conditions change.


ABOUT THE ROLE

We're looking for a Data Scientist with strong machine learning expertise and practical mining industry experience to help build and improve predictive models used across mining and mineral processing operations.

This is not a "train once and deploy" environment. You'll develop and maintain models that continuously adapt to changing geological conditions, ore variability, and operational differences across multiple mine sites. You'll work closely with domain experts and engineering teams to deliver measurable improvements in plant performance, recovery, and production outcomes. Mining operations often require ongoing model monitoring and adaptation because ore characteristics and process conditions evolve over time.


WHAT YOU'LL DO

 - Build, deploy, and improve machine learning models for mine-to-mill optimization

 - Analyze large-scale mining and processing datasets to identify operational improvement opportunities

 - Develop predictive models related to ore characteristics, fragmentation, recovery, flotation, throughput, and plant performance

 - Monitor model performance and address model drift across sites and changing geological conditions

 - Partner with mining engineers, metallurgists, and operations teams to translate business challenges into ML solutions

 - Work with structured and unstructured industrial datasets to support production decision-making

 - Design experiments and evaluate model performance in real operational environments

 - Contribute to MLOps and model monitoring practices for production systems


REQUIRED QUALIFICATIONS

 - 5+ years of experience in Data Science, Machine Learning, or Applied AI

 - Strong Python and machine learning fundamentals

 - Experience building production ML systems and maintaining models over time

 - Hands-on experience with:
   
   - Google Cloud Platform (GCP)
   
   - BigQuery
   
   - Parquet-based data pipelines
   
   - Model monitoring and performance tracking

 - Strong statistical modeling and experimentation skills

 - Experience working with large operational or industrial datasets

 - Excellent communication skills and ability to collaborate with cross-functional teams


STRONGLY PREFERRED

 - Direct experience in mining, mineral processing, metallurgy, or mine-to-mill optimization

 - Understanding of:
   
   - Ore variability
   
   - Rock hardness and fragmentation
   
   - Flotation processes
   
   - Recovery optimization
   
   - Mill performance drivers
   
   - Production process analytics

 - Experience supporting multiple operational sites with varying geological conditions

 - Experience with time-series modeling and industrial process optimization


NICE TO HAVE

 - Experience with MLOps frameworks

 - Knowledge of process control systems and industrial data platforms

 - Experience with predictive maintenance or optimization systems

 - Background in copper, gold, or base metals operations


COMPENSATION & BENEFITS

 - Competitive compensation (~USD $140,000/year, depending on experience)

 - Fully remote

 - Opportunity to work on cutting-edge AI applications in the mining industry

 - Small, highly technical team with direct impact on product and customer outcomes

 - Fast-moving hiring process


INTERVIEW PROCESS

 1. G2i recorded interview (experience review + targeted technical deep dive)

 2. Client interview with

source_jobs.title: Senior/Staff AI Engineer — Agentic Development (Next.js/Node.js)
source_jobs.description:
Contract | 40–80 hrs/week

Roles open on a rolling basis - apply to join the talent bench and we’ll reach out when one matches. Expect 40+ hrs once a project starts; timing depends on availability, but we move people in at the earliest genuine opportunity.

We're looking for senior-to-staff+ software engineers to build in a fast-moving, high-ambiguity environment with tight deadlines and demanding KPIs. This is hands-on engineering work for people who know how to move quickly and operate with minimal direction.



What You'll Be Doing

You'll be building agentic applications and tooling at the frontier of LLM-powered development. This means working directly with modern coding agents and LLM infrastructure, shipping fast in an environment where requirements shift, and the bar is high. You'll need to make strong judgment calls under pressure and deliver without hand-holding.



Tech Stack

The core stack is Next.js/Node.js with TypeScript throughout — typed end-to-end, no exceptions. You should be deeply fluent in this ecosystem rather than just familiar with it.



Who You Are

You're a minimum senior-level engineer, though staff+ level is ideal. You have hands-on agentic development experience, including practical knowledge of LLMs and familiarity with the tooling ecosystem for coding agents. You thrive in highly ambiguous environments, work well under tight deadlines, and are comfortable being measured against very high KPIs. You move fast without sacrificing quality.



Nice to Have

RAG application development experience is a plus. A background in reinforcement learning (RL) is also valuable.



Engagement Details

 - Hours: 40 minimum, up to 80 per week

 - Environment: High-ambiguity, deadline-driven, high-KPI

source_jobs.title: Data Engineer
source_jobs.description:
DATA ENGINEER (MINING & INDUSTRIAL DATA INTEGRATION)

Remote | South America Preferred (Chile, Brazil, Peru, Argentina, Colombia)
Full-Time | Direct Hire
Compensation: Up to USD $120,000/year (Flexible Based on Experience)


ABOUT THE COMPANY

Our client, Alloy Mining Intelligence, is building AI-powered systems that help mining operations optimize production and make better real-time decisions through industrial data and machine learning. The team combines deep expertise in mining operations, industrial data infrastructure, and applied AI to deliver measurable improvements across mining processes.

They are looking for a hands-on Data Engineer who can bridge the gap between operational mining systems and modern cloud-based data platforms.


THE OPPORTUNITY

This role is ideal for someone who enjoys solving real-world industrial data challenges.

You'll work directly with data generated from mining operations, integrating historian systems, sensor data, and operational datasets into cloud-native platforms that support advanced analytics and machine learning.

The work goes beyond traditional ETL. You'll help build the data backbone that enables optimization models and operational recommendations to flow back into industrial systems used by mine operators.


WHAT YOU'LL DO

 - Design and maintain data pipelines that ingest industrial and historian data into GCP.

 - Process and transform large-scale Parquet datasets generated from mining operations.

 - Load and optimize data within BigQuery and Bigtable environments.

 - Build reliable data integration workflows between operational technology (OT) systems and cloud infrastructure.

 - Support machine learning teams by providing clean, accessible, production-ready datasets.

 - Help operationalize recommendations generated by analytics and ML systems back into historian or control environments.

 - Collaborate closely with Data Scientists, Engineers, and mining domain experts.

 - Troubleshoot data quality, reliability, and performance issues across the data platform.


REQUIRED QUALIFICATIONS

 - 5+ years of experience in Data Engineering or Data Platform Engineering.

 - Strong experience with Google Cloud Platform (GCP).

 - Hands-on experience with:
   
   - BigQuery
   
   - Bigtable
   
   - Parquet
   
   - Docker

 - Experience building and maintaining production-grade ETL/ELT pipelines.

 - Strong SQL skills and experience working with large-scale datasets.

 - Experience integrating data from multiple operational systems.

 - Ability to work independently in a remote environment.


NICE TO HAVE

 - Experience in Mining, Metals, Energy, Manufacturing, Industrial IoT, or Process Industries.

 - Experience working with:
   
   - Industrial historians (OSIsoft PI, AVEVA Historian, Canary, etc.)
   
   - SCADA systems
   
   - Process control systems
   
   - Time-series industrial data

 - Experience supporting ML or AI-driven products.

 - Familiarity with industrial operations and production optimization workflows.

 - Spanish proficiency is a plus.


WHAT MAKES THIS ROLE INTERESTING

 - Direct impact on real-world mining operations.

 - Work at the intersection of industrial systems, cloud infrastructure, and AI.

 - Collaborate with a highly specialized team focused on operational optimization.

 - Opportunity to solve complex data integration challenges rarely seen in traditional SaaS environments.

 - Fast hiring process with direct access to technical and executive leadership.


INTERVIEW PROCESS

 1. G2i Recorded Interview
    
    - Professional background review
    
    - Targeted technical deep dive

 2. Client Interview
    
    - Conversation with the VP of Data Science

 3. Decision
    
    - Fast turnaround after final interview


LOCATION

Remote across South America. Candidates with mining industry experience from Chile, Peru, Brazil, Argentina, or other mining-focused regions are especially encouraged to apply.

Interested? Apply today and help build t

source_jobs.title: FullStack Engineer
source_jobs.description:
FULLSTACK PHP DEVELOPER (BACKEND FOCUS)



Location: United States (Fully Remote)
Job Type: Full-Time
Salary: Up to $125,000 Base + Benefits




ABOUT OUR CLIENT

Our client delivers cybersecurity-first managed IT services and technology solutions that keep businesses secure, stable, and productive. For over 30 years, CTMS has partnered with organizations across industries — including healthcare, manufacturing, legal, education, and professional services — providing proactive IT support, cloud solutions, cybersecurity, and strategic IT consulting.




ROLE OVERVIEW

We are seeking a highly skilled Fullstack Developer with a strong backend focus to join our team. The ideal candidate will be primarily responsible for building, enhancing, and maintaining PHP-based applications, with backend development as a core focus. This role works closely with cross-functional technical teams to deliver performant, scalable, and secure solutions that support CTMS’s managed services platform and internal tools.




WHAT YOU’LL DO

Backend Development (Primary)

 - Design, develop, and maintain server-side logic using PHP.

 - Work effectively across projects that may include older PHP versions and complex legacy systems.

 - Build and optimize database schemas, queries, and stored procedures with MySQL (deep experience required).

 - Implement and troubleshoot clustering, replication, and high-availability database scenarios.

Frontend Development (Supporting)

 - Contribute to frontend features using JavaScript where applicable.

 - Collaborate with UI/UX designers and engineers to build responsive interfaces.

Collaboration & Architecture

 - Participate in code reviews, design discussions, and architectural planning.

 - Help diagnose and resolve production issues, including performance bottlenecks.

 - Write clear, maintainable, and testable code following best practices.




MUST-HAVE QUALIFICATIONS

- Strong PHP expertise, including working knowledge of older PHP versions (important for existing CTMS systems)
- Advanced MySQL experience
- Cluster / high-availability database deployment experience
- Proficiency with JavaScript for frontend integration tasks
- Experience building secure, fault-tolerant backend systems
- Comfortable in a remote, collaborative environment




PREFERRED QUALIFICATIONS

- Experience with modern PHP frameworks (Laravel, Symfony, etc.)
- Familiarity with caching, search, message queues
- Knowledge of RESTful APIs and integration methods
- Experience with version control (Git), CI/CD pipelines, and automated testing
- Exposure to managed IT or cybersecurity products/platforms




WHAT WE OFFER

- Fully remote work with flexible scheduling
- Competitive base salary — up to $125,000
- Benefits (health, dental, vision, retirement, etc.)
- Opportunity to work with a security-first, strategic technology services team
- Collaborative, supportive work culture




WHO YOU ARE

You’re a seasoned backend-leaning Fullstack Developer who thrives solving complex backend challenges (especially in PHP & MySQL), enjoys learning from legacy systems, and can contribute to frontend functionality when needed. You take pride in building reliable and secure applications that support critical business operations.




APPLY TODAY

Submit your resume and a short cover letter detailing your relevant PHP, MySQL, and clustering experience.

source_jobs.title: Senior Software Engineer - AI Interaction Evaluator (Codex / Claude Code, up to $200/hr)
source_jobs.description:
SENIOR AI INTERACTION EVALUATOR (CODEX / CLAUDE CODE)

Contract | $50-200/hr | 10+ hrs/week | Project-based

Roles open on a rolling basis - apply to join the talent bench and we’ll reach out when one matches. Expect 40+ hrs once a project starts; timing depends on availability, but we move people in at the earliest genuine opportunity.

These roles are currently filled but we hire on a rolling basis as new projects open up. Apply now to join our talent bench — qualified candidates will be contacted directly when roles become available.

Check out this Loom video for more details! https://www.loom.com/share/b0d1b0bf24c44ae8b95dca84b9db60e5

We’re looking for highly experienced software engineer (SR+) to help evaluate the quality of interactions with modern coding agents such as OpenAI Codex and Claude Code.

This is not a traditional engineering role.

You won’t be writing production code.
You’ll be evaluating something harder: whether the model thinks like a great engineer.




WHAT THIS ROLE ACTUALLY IS

You will assess how AI coding agents behave in real-world scenarios — focusing on:

 - Whether the response makes sense

 - Whether the preamble and reasoning are useful

 - Whether the output reflects strong engineering judgment

 - Whether the interaction feels right to an experienced developer

This role is about engineering taste — not syntax correctness.




WHAT YOU’LL BE DOING

 - Evaluate AI-generated coding interactions end-to-end

 - Judge whether outputs are:
   
   - Useful
   
   - Correct (at a high level)
   
   - Aligned with how a strong engineer would think

 - Assess the quality of explanations and reasoning, not just code

 - Distinguish between different levels of response quality (e.g. what makes something a 2 vs 4)

 - Provide clear, opinionated feedback on:
   
   - What worked
   
   - What didn’t
   
   - What felt “off” or misleading

 - Help define what great looks like when interacting with tools like Cursor
   
   


WHAT WE MEAN BY “TASTE”

We’re specifically looking for engineers who can answer questions like:

 - Does this feel like something a strong engineer would actually say?

 - Is this explanation helpful, or just technically correct?

 - Is the model guiding the user well, or just dumping output?

 - Would this interaction build or erode trust?

You should be comfortable making subjective but rigorous judgments.




WHO YOU ARE

 - Staff / Principal-level engineer (or equivalent experience)

 - Strong background in one of the below:
   
   - TypeScript / JavaScript
   
   - Python

 - Hands-on experience using:
   
   - OpenAI Codex
   
   - Claude Code
   
   - Cursor

 - Deep familiarity with modern AI-assisted dev workflows

 - Able to evaluate code without needing to fully execute or deeply review every line

 - Comfortable giving direct, opinionated feedback

 - High bar for what “good engineering” looks like
   
   


NICE TO HAVE

 - Experience with tools like Cursor or similar AI-first IDEs

 - Prior exposure to prompt design or evaluation workflows

 - Experience mentoring senior engineers or defining engineering standards
   
   


ENGAGEMENT DETAILS

 - US and Canada up to $200/hr

 - EU and Latam up to $150/hr

 - Other locations up to $100/hr

 - Hours: ~10–20 hours/week

 - Duration: Ongoing — project-based

 - Process:
   
   - Take-home evaluation exercise
   
   - One behavioral interview

source_jobs.title: Support Engineer
source_jobs.description:
CLIENT SUPPORT ENGINEER (PAYMENTS & FINANCIAL INFRASTRUCTURE)

Remote (U.S.) | Full-Time



Our client is building next-generation financial infrastructure that enables real-time movement of digital money across traditional banking systems and digital asset networks. They are looking for a technically strong Client Support Engineer to serve as the primary support contact for partner financial institutions, helping diagnose, troubleshoot, and resolve operational and technical issues across their payments platform.

This role sits at the intersection of technical support, operations, and engineering, making it ideal for candidates who enjoy solving complex problems, working directly with customers, and improving support processes in fast-paced environments.




WHAT YOU'LL DO


SERVE AS THE PRIMARY TECHNICAL CONTACT FOR FINANCIAL INSTITUTION PARTNERS

 - Act as the first point of contact for partner banks and financial institutions experiencing technical or operational issues.

 - Investigate and resolve support requests related to API behavior, connectivity, payment processing, and operational workflows.

 - Communicate effectively with both technical and non-technical stakeholders throughout the resolution process.


MANAGE AND RESOLVE OPERATIONAL EXCEPTIONS

 - Monitor support queues and exception management systems.

 - Triage, investigate, and drive incidents to resolution.

 - Escalate complex issues to Engineering when necessary and coordinate follow-through until closure.

 - Conduct root cause analysis and document findings.


IMPROVE SUPPORT OPERATIONS

 - Create and maintain support documentation, runbooks, troubleshooting guides, and escalation procedures.

 - Identify recurring issues and work with Engineering and Product teams to improve reliability and reduce support volume.

 - Contribute to the design and scaling of support processes, monitoring practices, and operational workflows.


COLLABORATE CROSS-FUNCTIONALLY

 - Partner closely with Engineering, Product, and Operations teams.

 - Provide actionable feedback based on customer and operational trends.

 - Help shape a scalable support function within a growing fintech environment.




REQUIRED QUALIFICATIONS

 - 3+ years of experience in a client-facing Support Engineering, Technical Support Engineering, Solutions Engineering, or Production Support role.

 - Experience supporting customers in fintech, payments, banking, financial infrastructure, or related industries.

 - Strong troubleshooting and root cause analysis skills.

 - Experience working directly with APIs, including testing and debugging REST APIs using tools such as Postman.

 - Familiarity with monitoring and observability platforms such as Grafana, Datadog, Splunk, or similar.

 - Working knowledge of Linux command line and networking fundamentals.

 - Excellent written and verbal communication skills.

 - Ability to manage multiple support cases independently and drive issues to resolution.




NICE-TO-HAVE QUALIFICATIONS

 - Experience supporting banks, payment processors, or financial institutions.

 - Knowledge of payment systems, money movement, banking infrastructure, or financial operations.

 - Experience with blockchain technology, digital assets, stablecoins, tokenized deposits, or cryptocurrency platforms.

 - Familiarity with automation and configuration management tools such as Ansible, Chef, or Terraform.

 - Experience working in startup or high-growth environments.




WHAT SUCCESS LOOKS LIKE

 - Support tickets are resolved accurately and efficiently.

 - Partner institutions receive timely and professional technical support.

 - Operational incidents are investigated thoroughly and documented clearly.

 - Support processes become increasingly scalable through automation, documentation, and continuous improvement.

 - Strong collaboration exists between Support, Engineering, and Product teams to improve platform reliability and customer experience.




BENEFITS

 - Competitive

source_jobs.title: Staff Fullstack Engineer - Camber Health
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT US:

Camber builds software to improve the quality and accessibility of healthcare. We streamline and replace manual work so clinicians can focus on what they do best: providing great care. For more details on our thesis, check out our write-up: What is Camber? https://camberhealth.notion.site/What-is-Camber-1a6c4e2fa5318049b6beef91b8ed7d1a?pvs=4

We’ve raised $50M in funding from phenomenal supporters at a16z, Craft Ventures, YCombinator, Manresa, and many others who are committed to improving the accessibility of care. For more information, take a look at: Announcing Camber https://www.camber.health/knowledge/launch




ABOUT OUR CULTURE:

Our mission to change behavioral health starts with us and how we operate. We don’t want to just change behavioral health, we want to change the way startups operate. Here are a few tactical examples:

1) Improving accessibility and quality of healthcare is something we live and breathe. Everyone on Camber’s team cares deeply about helping clinicians and patients.

2) We have to have a sense of humor. Healthcare is so broken, it's depressing if you don't laugh with us.




ABOUT THE ROLE:

Camber is looking for engineers who want to make healthcare more accessible and affordable. In this role, you’ll work across our stack, propose and lead new projects, and help us define the company’s technical and product direction.




WHAT YOU'LL DO:

 - Lead projects: You’ll take projects from 0 to 1. You’ll identify problems, build alignment, and implement solutions autonomously.

 - Write great code: Our engineers strive to write code that’s not only functional, but readable, maintainable, and scalable.

 - Build team culture: We believe that a great company and engineering team culture are essential to build great product




WHAT WE'RE LOOKING FOR:

 - Experience: 5+ years of experience working on production systems.

 - Mission-driven mindset: Driven to empower clinicians with product experiences that lets them focus on providing care, not manual check-the-box work.

 - Customer Empathy: Our engineers need to understand our customers’ pain points to build products that meet their needs.

 - Curiosity: We look for engineers who love learning new things. We don’t expect you to know everything about the technologies we’re using or our industry, as long as you’re curious and interested in learning.




Camber is based in New York City, and we prioritize in-person and hybrid candidates. Please refer to the top of this page for this position's hiring location.

Building an inclusive culture is one of our core tenets as a company. We’re very aware of structural inequalities that exist, and recognize that underrepresented minorities are less likely to apply for a role if they don’t think they meet all of the requirements. If that’s you and you’re reading this, we’d like to encourage you to apply regardless — we’d love to get to know you and see if there’s a place for you here!

In addition, we take security seriously, and all of our employees contribute to uphold security requirements and maintain compliance with HIPAA security regulations.

source_jobs.title: Staff Engineer
source_jobs.description:
LOOM OVERVIEW
WATCH THIS LOOM VIDEO http://loom.com/share/fe621cd1991e4c0fa21cac591caab141 FROM OUR CEO, GABE GREENBERG, https://x.com/gabe_g2i?lang=en FOR MORE DETAILS ABOUT THE ROLE!




ROLE OVERVIEW



At G2i, we don’t just hire for AI — we build with it. We partner with leading AI labs and technical founders to create next-generation developer platforms, data tools, and operational dashboards that power the AI frontier.

As a Staff Engineer, you’ll set technical direction and raise the execution bar while building a high-performing team. You’ll guide architecture and delivery across modern full-stack systems (React 19 + Node.js), build durable engineering practices, and ensure we ship fast without sacrificing quality.





KEY RESPONSIBILITIES



 - Lead and grow an engineering team: hiring, coaching, performance management, career development.

 - Set and evolve technical strategy for developer platforms, dashboards, and internal tooling.

 - Partner with Product/Design to deliver roadmap outcomes with high velocity and strong craftsmanship.

 - Establish engineering standards: code quality, testing, documentation, security, and operational excellence.

 - Own delivery health: sprint/iteration hygiene, predictable execution, and strong on-call/incident practices.

 - Drive architecture decisions across front-end, back-end (tRPC encouraged), and deployment (Docker + CI/CD).

 - Build a culture of ownership, feedback, and continuous improvement across teams.
   
   


IDEAL QUALIFICATIONS



 - 7+ years building software, including 2–4+ years leading teams (EM/Tech Lead/Head of Eng).

 - Strong full-stack understanding (React + Node.js), modern front-end patterns, SSR/performance, API design.

 - Experience with shipping and operating production systems (cloud, CI/CD, observability, incident management).

 - Proven ability to balance speed with quality in startup environments.

 - Clear communicator with strong judgment and a track record of developing engineers.
   
   


COMPENSATION & TERMS



 - 120 – 200USD/Hr, based on experience and location

 - Contract leadership roles considered (up to 6 months)

 - Remote-first, global team
   
   


APPLICATION PROCESS



Resume submission → Leadership/technical screen → → Behavioral interview → Offer




ABOUT G2I



G2i helps startups hire senior engineers faster and smarter through video-based technical evaluation. We’ve placed thousands of developers worldwide since 2016 and built a community rooted in Quality, Urgency, and Agility.

source_jobs.title: Senior Full Stack Engineer (backend leaning)
source_jobs.description:
About the Role

Our client is an early-stage, venture-backed fintech startup on a mission to expand access to financial tools for people historically excluded from the traditional credit system. They're growing fast, capital-efficient, and looking for a Backend Engineer to help build and scale the infrastructure powering their products.

This is a high-ownership role for someone who wants to own systems end-to-end, move fast, and lean heavily into AI-assisted development to multiply their output without sacrificing quality.

What You'll Do

 - Own backend systems and infrastructure end-to-end, from design through deployment and scaling

 - Build performant, reliable APIs and services that power core financial products

 - Design and implement scalable architectures on AWS using Node (TypeScript)

 - Make pragmatic tradeoffs between speed, reliability, scalability, and maintainability

 - Ship production code frequently while maintaining high quality standards

 - Help establish technical patterns and best practices for the engineering team

 - Interface effectively with product engineers and other stakeholders

What We're Looking For

 - Bachelor's degree in Computer Science or a related technical field

 - 5+ years of professional software engineering experience, with a significant backend focus

 - Experience building and scaling production systems with Node and AWS

 - Proficiency with TypeScript strongly preferred

 - Track record of delivering reliable, performant systems at scale

 - Systems-oriented mindset with a deep focus on performance, reliability, and scalability

 - Strong sense of ownership and accountability for system outcomes

 - Comfortable thinking critically about architectural decisions and their long-term implications

 - Thrives in ambiguity and can make sound technical decisions with incomplete information

 - Self-starter who can work independently and drive projects to completion

 - Embraces AI-assisted development to accelerate output while maintaining high code quality

What to Expect

 - High ownership with minimal bureaucracy

 - A fast-moving environment where roles and priorities evolve quickly

 - An intense pace of execution and growth — this is not a 9-to-5, work-life-balance-optimized role

 - Speed, learning, and impact as core values

Compensation

Salary range of 150-250k USD/year + equity, depending on location and seniority.

source_jobs.title: Senior Engineer
source_jobs.description:
LOOM OVERVIEW
WATCH THIS LOOM VIDEO http://loom.com/share/fe621cd1991e4c0fa21cac591caab141 FROM OUR CEO, GABE GREENBERG, https://x.com/gabe_g2i?lang=en FOR MORE DETAILS ABOUT THE ROLE!




ROLE OVERVIEW



At G2i, we don’t just hire for AI — we build with it. We’re partnering with leading AI labs and technical founders to create next-generation developer platforms, data tools, and operational dashboards that power the AI frontier.

As a Full-Stack Developer, you’ll architect and build the systems that make this innovation possible — fast, scalable, and elegant. Using React 19, Node.js, you’ll deliver products that balance cutting-edge technology with developer-focused precision.




KEY RESPONSIBILITIES



 - Design, develop, and maintain full-stack web applications using React 19, and Node.js.

 - Implement tRPC APIs and ensure type-safe, end-to-end communication across services.

 - Containerize and deploy applications using Docker, ensuring smooth CI/CD pipelines.

 - Collaborate with designers and other engineers to ship performant, scalable features quickly.

 - Maintain high code quality, test coverage, and documentation standards.
   
   


IDEAL QUALIFICATIONS



 - 5+ years of professional full-stack experience (or equivalent).

 - Proven experience in React 19, Node.js,

 - Hands-on experience with Docker and cloud-based deployment workflows.

 - Startup experience — you’re fast-moving, pragmatic, and adaptable.

 - Strong understanding of modern front-end patterns, server-side rendering, and performance optimization.
   
   


COMPENSATION & TERMS



 - 50 - 150USD/Hr based on experience and location.

 - Contract roles available, up to 6 months

 - Remote-first, global team.
   
   


APPLICATION PROCESS



 1. Resume submission.

 2. Technical challenge.

 3. Behavioral test.

 4. Offer.
    
    


ABOUT G2I



G2i helps startups hire senior engineers faster and smarter through video-based technical evaluation. We’ve placed thousands of developers worldwide since 2016 and built a community rooted in Quality, Urgency, and Agility.

notes: Highly technical job postings with a focus on AI, data, and software engineering. Many roles are remote and full-time.
